title: Earthy Crumbly German Pork Knuckle Crockpot Creme Fraiche
description: This brunch-mood dish poaches tender pork knuckle in a rich creme fraiche sauce with earthy vegetables for a comforting and egg-free-friendly meal.
instructions: Rinse pork knuckle and pat dry. Slice onion, dice leek, cut carrots into thin sticks, slice turnips.
In a saucepan over medium heat, add oil. Brown meat on all sides, then set aside.
Sauté diced onions in the same pan until translucent. Add leeks, carrots, and turnips; cook for 5 minutes.
Stir in tomato paste with some flour to make a roux. Slowly pour in water while stirring continuously.
Reduce heat to low and add white wine vinegar, salt, pork knuckle, and all vegetables. Simmer gently for about 2 hours until tender.
In a separate pan, heat creme fraiche over medium heat; stir occasionally until slightly thickened.
Pour the stewed mixture into a slow cooker with the creme fraiche. Cover and cook on low for another hour.
Serve hot with crusty bread or as part of a brunch spread.
